IICRC Certified · Structural Timber Drying · Auckland-Wide

Structural Timber Drying Service

Auckland's wet winters regularly push timber moisture content above the allowable threshold. Framing must reach ≤20% MC before wall linings can be installed and your building inspection can proceed. Waiting for it to dry naturally can cost weeks on your programme.

Structural timber drying is a service we specialise in. With over 10 years in the industry, we've developed what we believe to be a process at the cutting edge of getting frames dry in the quickest possible timeframe. Our method is grounded in the IICRC S500 Standard for Professional Water Damage Restoration and the IICRC Applied Structural Drying Technician programme — internationally recognised benchmarks for effective, evidence-based moisture remediation. — IICRC S500, 5th Edition; IICRC ASD Programme

Central to our process is the use of Zipwall drying chambers — a controlled-environment approach that concentrates airflow and dehumidification directly around affected framing. This method is not widely used in Auckland, and we believe it will be a genuine game changer for builders and contractors who can't afford to wait. If you think we can help, don't hesitate to get in touch.

The Problem

Wet Framing Holds Your Build Up.

Timber framing must reach ≤20% MC before wall linings can be installed and your building inspection can proceed. Below are the moisture content thresholds we work to under New Zealand building standards.

The Numbers We Work To

Moisture Content Standards

20%

Maximum Before Lining

Timber framing must be at or below 20% MC before wall linings can be installed — the threshold to pass your building inspection and close up the wall.

NZ Building Code regulations — Red Stag Timber technical documentation & NZ Building Performance guidance
18%

Long-Term In-Service Standard

NZS 3604:2011 specifies that structural timber design solutions apply only where moisture content does not exceed 18% throughout the building's design life.

NZS 3604:2011 — Clause 2.3.4 & NZS 3602:1995 — Standards New Zealand
Compliance & Certification

We Work to the Standard. We Document Everything.

IICRC Certification

Structural Timber Drying

Our technicians hold IICRC certification in structural timber drying — the international benchmark for professional moisture remediation.

NZS 3604:2011

Timber-Framed Buildings Standard

All drying targets comply with NZS 3604:2011 — New Zealand's primary standard governing the design and construction of timber-framed buildings.

Auckland Council

Code of Practice Compliant

Work carried out consistent with Auckland Council code of practice requirements, including full-penetration moisture mapping and a written compliance report on request.

How It Works

A Tested Process. No Fan Heaters.

We build a sealed Zipwall drying chamber around the affected framing, then run air movers and commercial dehumidification to drive moisture out fast. Every job is checked every 24 hours and fully documented.

01

Moisture Mapping

Full-penetration testing across all affected framing. Readings and photos to establish a baseline.

02

Zipwall Chamber

Sealed drying chamber built using Zipwall barriers — isolating the area to concentrate airflow and dehumidification.

03

Air Movers + Dehumidifier

2 × air movers and a 90L commercial dehumidifier drive moisture out of the timber continuously.

04

24-Hour Monitoring

Daily site visits — checking readings, adjusting equipment, photo-documenting the reduction.

05

Final Report

Full before/after moisture report — ready for your building inspector or council.

The Kit

What's Included in Every Job

Zipwall Chamber

Sealed barrier system built around the affected area to contain and concentrate the drying environment.

2 × Air Movers

High-velocity air circulation driving moisture out of the timber and into the chamber for extraction.

1 × 90L Dehumidifier

Commercial-grade unit continuously extracting moisture from the air inside the sealed environment.

Moisture Mapping & Reports

Full-penetration readings documented with photos at every 24-hour visit. Final report on completion.
